full_text:
Temperature in MP, Chattisgarh and Rajasthan reaches over 40°C:Rain and snowfall expected in mountains
- Copy link
The weather across the country is once again showing different patterns. States in North and Central India are experiencing intense heat, while hilly areas may see rain and snowfall due to the influence of a Western Disturbance.
The weather pattern across the country is changing rapidly. In many states of North, West and Central India, temperatures have started rising with the beginning of March. In Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Haryana, daytime temperatures have moved above normal, and in many cities the mercury is reaching close to 35 to 40 degrees Celsius.
In Rajasthan, hot winds are blowing due to the impact of an anti-cyclone formed in the Arabian Sea, causing temperatures to rise quickly. Meanwhile, in Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Haryana, daytime temperatures have increased by 2 to 3 degrees in the past few days, and strong heat is being felt in many cities.
On the other hand, weather conditions in the hilly states are expected to change. With a Western Disturbance becoming active in Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand, rain and snowfall are likely in higher altitude areas in the coming days.
Next 2 days weather update
March 7–8 – Rain and snowfall are likely in Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand. Light rain is expected in West Bengal, Sikkim and Odisha.
Weather condition of states…
Rajasthan: Heatwave likely in the state for the next two weeks, with temperatures exceeding 35°C, with temperatures rising in several cities, including Bhilwara and Kota.
The heat is steadily increasing in Rajasthan. On Thursday, more than 15 cities in the state recorded maximum temperatures above 35°C. Daytime temperatures have risen in Bhilwara, Alwar, Pilani, Kota, and several other cities. Heatwave conditions are expected in some areas over the next two weeks due to the intense heat.
Madhya Pradesh: March-like heat in the state, mercury to reach 40°C in the next 4 days
Madhya Pradesh is experiencing April-like heat in March. Daytime temperatures have crossed 39°C in many cities. Temperatures could exceed 40°C in the next four days. On Thursday, the daytime temperature in Narmadapuram reached 39°C.
Himachal Pradesh: Western Disturbance likely to become active from March 7; rain and snowfall expected in high mountains
A Western Disturbance is expected to become active in Himachal Pradesh from March 7. Its effects will continue until March 11. Light rain and snowfall are expected in higher areas tomorrow. The weather will remain clear on March 8, but rain and snowfall are likely again from March 9 to 11.

full_text:
Scorching Heat in Rajasthan: IMD Warns of Rising Temperatures and Possible Heatwave
Rajasthan Weather: The heat wave in Rajasthan is steadily increasing, with temperatures in many cities reaching above 35 degrees Celsius. More than 15 cities, including Bhilwara, Alwar, Pilani, and Kota, have recorded increases in daytime temperatures.
The heat wave in Rajasthan continues to intensify. On Thursday, there were over 15 cities in the state where the high temperature during the day was above 35 degrees Celsius. The high temperatures during the day increased in cities such as Bhilwara, Alwar, Pilani, Kota, etc.
The Meteorological Center at Jaipur has predicted that the state is likely to experience more heat in the coming days, as the temperature is likely to rise in the coming days. The state is also likely to experience a heatwave in some areas during the coming days. The state is likely to experience clear weather in the coming week. The state experienced clear weather in most areas during the last 24 hours, with the sun shining from the morning itself, resulting in high temperatures during the day. The maximum temperature during the day was above 36 degrees Celsius in cities such as Udaipur, Sriganganagar, Pilani, Phalodi, Kota, Jodhpur, Jaisalmer, Churu,
With the exception of Bikaner, Hanumangarh, Jaipur, Jalore, Jodhpur, and Phalodi, minimum temperatures remained below 20 degrees Celsius in most parts of the state. Night temperatures in Alwar, Baran, Bhilwara, Karauli, and Sirohi fell below 15 degrees Celsius.
According to the Meteorological Department, temperatures are expected to rise by 2 to 3 degrees Celsius over the next week. During this period, the state will experience clear skies, bright sunshine, and mild, warm winds are expected during the day in the western districts. Additionally, due to the influence of an anticyclone in the Arabian Sea, maximum temperatures may remain 4 to 8 degrees Celsius above normal. There is a strong possibility that the temperature will reach 40 degrees Celsius in some areas of the state between March 8 and 10.
